task: T0.1-T0.4 phase-0 stopgaps: five/seven drift, presenter tool name, slug prep, /research-init command shell
went_well: grep-driven sweep found all four drift sites in one pass; consistency now CI-counted, not asserted
went_wrong: the drift survived 4 commits of human review before this — pure-prose facts need a script counting them

task: T0.5 CI skeleton: lint_agents + check_templates + check_consistency + growth-tree/fleet-status jobs
went_well: check_consistency counts agents/*.md instead of trusting docs — the T0.1 bug class is now structurally dead
went_wrong: Windows MAX_PATH broke repo scans mid-build (repo path ~200 chars deep); scripts needed \\?\ long-path fallbacks

task: T1.1 fleet_status.py C1-C7 gate-invariant checker + destructive test on demo copy
went_well: contracts were precise enough to code the invariants against without inventing fields
went_wrong: demo project lacked trace/manifest files for its own claimed states — the example itself was gate-violating until backfilled

task: T1.2 optional hooks: H1 claim-gate PreToolUse blocker, H3 stop-status reminder, SessionStart gate summary
went_well: reconstructing post-edit content in the hook lets fleet_status judge the proposed state, not the stale disk state
went_wrong: H2 (auditor-only markers) unshippable — hook layer cannot identify the calling agent; boundary documented instead of faked

task: T1.3 method-card contract: Methods channel through the writer firewall
went_well: copied-never-remembered generalized cleanly from results to hyperparameters
went_wrong: engineer's blanket paper/ write-ban conflicted with card drafting; needed an explicit single-exception carve-out

task: T1.4+T1.5 cross-model audit interface + contracts pinned into projects (.fleet/contracts/, versioned)
went_well: verdict-first-then-brief ordering kills anchoring; Reviewers: header makes single-model coverage visible downstream

task: T2.1-T2.3 --minimal init variant, tiered deck review, leader drift self-check line
went_well: cut-ceremony-keep-gates line held: all six gates survive --minimal untouched

task: T2.4 README rewrite (en + zh-CN, FAQ, enforcement-layers section)
went_well: zh-CN written as native prose instead of translated English — the user explicitly flagged translationese as the defect
went_wrong: feature list churn means README now has 6 places that must track reality; consistency CI covers only agent counts
